name: Cattaraugus County Employees
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving Cattaraugus County employees with deposit accounts, loans, and financial services. NCUA-insured with real-time home banking.
description_long:
Cattaraugus County Employees Federal Credit Union (CCEFCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving employees and eligible groups in Cattaraugus County, New York. Operating from their headquarters at 100 Fair Oak Street in Little Valley, the credit union has been providing financial services to its community for decades and recently underwent a merger to strengthen operations. As a federally chartered credit union, CCEFCU is regulated and insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), providing members with deposit insurance protection up to $250,000 per account category.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, Christmas Club accounts, share certificates (CDs), and investment accounts on the deposit side. For borrowing needs, CCEFCU provides personal loans, home equity loans, vehicle loans (new and used), recreational loans, credit builder programs, and first mortgages through OwnersChoice Funding. Members also have access to Visa debit cards, payroll services with direct deposit and payroll deduction options, and online home banking with real-time account access. Additional services include notary public, money orders, discount tickets, and voice response units for account inquiries.

CCEFCU distinguishes itself as a membership-based institution with eligibility tied to Cattaraugus County employment or group affiliation. The credit union emphasizes member relationship banking with competitive rates on both loans and savings products. Their real-time home banking platform provides modern digital access, and the organization actively communicates with members about account maintenance, such as beneficiary updates and address changes—demonstrating attention to member service quality and financial planning needs.

The main limitation is membership eligibility restricted to Cattaraugus County employees and qualifying groups, making it unavailable to the general public. The website mentions ongoing merger integration, which may create temporary operational transitions. Like most community credit unions, CCEFCU may have more limited product depth compared to larger national financial institutions, though their core offerings cover standard consumer and mortgage lending adequately.
pros: NCUA-insured deposits up to $250,000 per account category provide federal protection
Real-time home banking platform allows members to access and manage accounts 24/7
Competitive rates on loans and savings products compared to traditional banks
First mortgage lending through partnership with OwnersChoice Funding
Member-owned structure means profits potentially returned to members rather than shareholders
Comprehensive loan options including credit builder loans to help members build credit history
Direct deposit and payroll deduction services for convenient account funding
Personalized member service with emphasis on account maintenance (beneficiary updates, address changes)
cons: Membership limited to Cattaraugus County employees and eligible groups—not open to general public
Ongoing merger may cause service disruptions or operational changes during integration
Limited branch network compared to national banks or large credit unions
Smaller institution may have fewer advanced digital features or mobile app capabilities
Product offerings appear more basic compared to credit unions or banks in larger metropolitan areas
best_for: Cattaraugus County government and municipal employees seeking member-owned financial institution
Individuals wanting home equity loans and mortgages with personalized community banking service
Members building credit history who qualify for credit builder loan programs
Families prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and competitive savings rates over national brand options
diagnosis: Best for Cattaraugus County employees and eligible group members seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive rates, mortgage lending, and personalized service. Primary limitation is membership eligibility restriction to county employees and qualifying organizations, making it inaccessible to the general public. Current merger integration should be confirmed as complete before opening new accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
